reproducible_artifact: A 'two-disciplines audit' template for engineering quarterly planning. (1) List the architectural-reading work for the quarter (RFCs to be written, design-doc reviews to be held, abstraction-leak audits to be performed). (2) List the shipping-discipline work for the quarter (deploy-cadence target, post-mortem quality criteria, on-call rotation, rollback hygiene reviews). (3) Verify the two lists have separate calendar slots, separate review forums (architecture review channel vs incident review), and separate success metrics. If they collapse into one budget, name the collapse explicitly and split before quarter-start.
verdict_reason: The engineering layer's claim is reducible to a concrete planning artifact (the two-disciplines audit). The implication directly states the artifact ('Name the two disciplines on your team explicitly: the architectural reading and the shipping discipline. Plan calendar, headcount, and review forums for each separately'). The artifact is reproducible across organizations because the META act (naming two disciplines distinctly) is independent of the specific tools or scale.
